feed.title: Functional Programming Weekly
feed.subtitle: Exploring pure functions, monads, and type theory

entry.title: Algebraic Effects: Beyond Monads
entry.summary: Algebraic effects offer a composable alternative to monad transformers for managing side effects in functional programs.
entry.content:
Algebraic effects have emerged as a promising approach to managing computational effects. Unlike monads, which require explicit transformer stacking, algebraic effects allow handlers to be composed freely.

Languages like Koka, Eff, and OCaml 5 have adopted effect systems, showing that the idea scales to real-world use.

entry.title: Dependent Types for Everyday Programming
entry.summary: Can dependent types move from academic curiosity to practical tool?
entry.content: Dependent types allow types to depend on values, enabling proofs at the type level. Idris 2 makes this practical.

entry.title: The Monad Tutorial Fallacy
entry.summary: Why yet another monad tutorial probably won't help beginners.
